On July 28, about 600,000 people came to hear legendary bands like The Grateful Dead play. It was the largest audience ever for a pop festival at that time.
activity_question: Which famous band played at the 'Summer Jam' festival?
follow_up_question: Can you imagine being in a crowd that big? How do you think it would feel?

title: Jerry Lee Lewis Rocks TV Debut
story: Fingers flying across the keys, Jerry Lee Lewis set the stage on fire with his electrifying piano skills. The rock and roll sensation made his television debut in 1957, performing a wild version of 'Whole Lotta Shakin' Goin' On' on The Steve Allen Show. On July 28, viewers got their first taste of Lewis's high-energy style that would soon make him a music legend.
activity_question: What song did Jerry Lee Lewis perform on his TV debut?
bonus_fact: Jerry Lee Lewis was nicknamed 'The Killer' for his powerful and energetic performances.

title: Telstar-3A: Beaming Messages from Space
story: Whoosh! A powerful rocket blasted off, carrying a special satellite into orbit. On July 28, 1983, NASA launched Telstar-3A, a cutting-edge communications marvel. This space-age wonder helped beam TV shows, phone calls, and data across vast distances. Like a cosmic postman, Telstar-3A linked people around the world, making our planet feel a little smaller.
activity_question: What did Telstar-3A help beam across vast distances?
bonus_fact: Telstar satellites were named after a hit song about communication satellites!

title: Mariner I's Splash Landing
story: Whoosh! A rocket blasted off, aiming for Venus. But something went wrong. Mariner I, a space probe, tumbled back to Earth on July 28, 1962. Instead of soaring to the stars, it splashed into the Atlantic Ocean. This unexpected swim taught scientists valuable lessons for future missions.
activity_question: What happened to Mariner I?
activity_choices: It fell into the Atlantic Ocean
activity_answer: It fell into the Atlantic Ocean
follow_up_question: What planet was Mariner I supposed to explore?

title: Lights Out in the Windy City
story: Zap! Chicago plunged into darkness. On July 28, 1990, a massive power outage struck the bustling city. Millions of people found themselves without electricity. Elevators stopped, traffic lights went dark, and air conditioners fell silent. Despite the chaos, Chicagoans came together, helping neighbors and staying calm until power was restored.
activity_question: What happened to Chicago in 1990?
follow_up_question: How might you stay safe during a blackout?

title: Rock and Roll's New Home Takes Shape
story: Guitars strummed a victory tune as the final steel beam found its place on the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum in Cleveland, Ohio. This milestone in 1994 marked a big step toward creating a shrine for music legends. The uniquely shaped building, resembling a record player, would soon house treasures from rock history. On July 28, construction crews celebrated this high note in the museum's creation.
activity_question: What type of beam was placed on the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum?
bonus_fact: The museum officially opened to the public about a year later, in September 1995.

title: Flames Engulf Beloved Seaside Pier
story: Disaster struck a popular seaside attraction in 2008. On July 28, fire raced through the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare, England. Flames devoured the wooden structure, leaving only charred remains. Amazingly, this wasn't the first time. The same pier had burned down decades earlier, but the town rebuilt it. Now, they faced the challenge again.
activity_question: What happened to the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare?
follow_up_question: How did the town respond to the fire?
